# UC-13.6.2 · Grafana Data Source Query Error Rate by Type (Prometheus Loki Influx CloudWatch SQL Splunk Elasticsearch Tempo Mimir Pyroscope correlation with audit and HTTP 4xx 5xx proxy paths)

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Reliability, Availability &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*Picture every dashboard as a wall of televisions, each fed by a different cable box in another building. We watch which cables glitch, which keys stop working, and which stations go silent so repair crews know exactly which box to fix before the room thinks the whole grid died.*

---

## Description

Detects Grafana data source query failures broken out by data source type and organization or dashboard identifiers by correlating Grafana server error logs, audit trails, native Grafana Prometheus metrics, and reverse-proxy HTTP status lines so teams find blank panels and provider outages before user reports converge.

## Value

Reliability leaders shorten mean time to innocence when a single chart goes blank because they can see whether Prometheus timeouts, Loki guardrails, CloudWatch regional stress, SQL pool exhaustion, Splunk quota language, or OAuth drift drives the failure, while finance-facing stakeholders receive an evidence trail that ties outages to owning squads through inventory joins rather than anecdotal screenshots.

## Implementation

Land `grafana:server`, `grafana:audit` or `grafana:json`, Prometheus-style Grafana metrics, and ingress access sourcetypes into observability indexes; publish `grafana_datasources_inventory.csv` and a catalog `grafana_datasource_owner` feed; save `observability_uc_13_6_2_grafana_ds_query_error_rate` on a five-to-fifteen minute cadence with earliest four hours; route critical and high severities to platform observability on-call; archive weekly evidence CSV snapshots to a restricted index.

## Evidence

Saved search `observability_uc_13_6_2_grafana_ds_query_error_rate` on a five or fifteen minute schedule; observability dashboard drilldowns across Grafana server, audit, metrics, and proxy sourcetypes; alert routing to platform observability on-call; weekly CSV exports archived to a restricted evidence index.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In a lab Grafana stack, misconfigure a Prometheus data source to an unreachable endpoint, refresh panels to generate `lvl=eror` `tsdb` lines and matching proxy errors, and confirm `observability_uc_13_6_2_grafana_ds_query_error_rate` returns critical or high severity within two evaluation windows with non-null `recommended_action`.

### Negative scenario

After restoring valid credentials and healthy backends with quiet audit trails, steady proxy 200 responses, and balanced Grafana metrics labels, confirm severities remain low or informational and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to critical and high only.

## Detailed Implementation

### Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with site reliability engineers who run Grafana fleets, observability backends, reverse proxies in front of Grafana, and the identity systems that mint OAuth tokens and API keys for data source plugins. This use case answers whether Grafana can faithfully query Prometheus, Loki, InfluxDB, CloudWatch, SQL engines, Splunk, Elasticsearch, Tempo, Mimir, Pyroscope, and adjacent tracing stores at the moment panels render, not whether a single exporter is up on a distant node. Blank panels, incident-window gaps, and silent partial outages usually arrive as correlated bursts across Grafana server logs, audit narratives, native Grafana metrics, and ingress access lines that show HTTP semantics for proxied backend calls.

Grafana OSS, Grafana Enterprise, and Grafana Cloud share the same conceptual split between the control plane that stores dashboards, folders, organizations, and data source definitions and the data plane where `tsdb.*` loggers record query execution against remote systems. Enterprise adds fine-grained access control, signed audit exports, and reporting features that change where audit events land but not the fundamental need to join `org_id`, folder UIDs, dashboard UIDs, panel identifiers, and `datasource_uid` into one row. Cloud introduces multi-tenant rate limits, managed data stores, and regional placement that can surface as `429` storms on the proxy path while self-managed stacks still show clean CPU graphs on Grafana pods.

Log semantics you should teach every responder include `logger=tsdb.prometheus`, `logger=tsdb.cloudwatch`, `logger=tsdb.loki`, and `logger=secrets.kvstore` when secure credential storage fails to unwrap secrets before a query leaves Grafana. Grafana’s file log format often prints `lvl=eror` for error severity even though the string looks like a typo; your parsers must not normalize that away or this detection arm goes quiet during real incidents. HTTP paths of interest include `/api/datasources/proxy/` segments that appear in nginx, Envoy, HAProxy, or AWS Application Load Balancer access logs when Grafana fronts queries through its API surface instead of direct browser calls.

Shipping strategies are threefold and may coexist. First, tail Grafana server logs with a Splunk Universal Forwarder `monitor://` stanza on Linux VMs or read container stdout when Grafana runs in Kubernetes, routing to an index such as `grafana` or `observability` with `sourcetype=grafana:server` after `props.conf` line breaking and timestamp extraction. Second, stream logs through Grafana Loki and a Promtail or OpenTelemetry Collector `loki` exporter that forwards JSON to Splunk HTTP Event Collector when you already standardized on Loki as an intermediate. Third, use an OpenTelemetry Collector `filelog` receiver pointed at mounted log paths plus an `splunk_hec` exporter for organizations that want one agent for traces, metrics, and logs. Audit logs may use `sourcetype=grafana:audit` when exported as newline JSON or `sourcetype=grafana:json` when multiplexed with other Grafana JSON streams; normalize `action`, `username`, `orgId`, and resource identifiers before this search runs.

Metrics from Grafana’s Prometheus endpoint should ride the same OpenTelemetry Collector path your platform already uses for UC-13.3.2 style metric hygiene: scrape `grafana_metrics` locally, translate exemplars and histogram buckets carefully, and ship to Splunk metrics indexes with consistent `metric_name` strings such as `grafana_datasource_request_total` and `grafana_datasource_request_duration_seconds_bucket`. Preserve labels that identify `datasource_type`, plugin identifiers, and status outcomes so the multisearch metrics arm can separate failing series from successful ones without double counting canary scrapes twice.

RBAC and privacy reviews must settle which dashboard titles, user names, and backend connection strings may appear in evidence packs. Redact connection URLs in lookup tables, store only UIDs in inventory CSV files, and restrict roles that can read high-cardinality proxy logs. Break-glass credentials belong in vaults with rotation telemetry correlated to this alert; never paste live API tokens into lookup files checked into git.

Differentiation recap: this is not UC-13.6.1 dashboard load latency, which measures how fast HTML and JSON return to browsers. It is not pure Prometheus target scrape health without Grafana context. It is the fused story of query failures by data source family, organization, folder or dashboard identifiers when present, enriched with audit and HTTP status classes so responders separate timeout pressure from authentication drift from schema mismatch.

Risk briefing: canary dashboards that intentionally hit failing backends will move error rates without customer impact; gate them with lookup metadata. Large organizations run hundreds of data sources; cardinality controls on `datasource_uid` and bucketing `_time` to five minutes keep search modes bounded. Legal hold on audit indexes may extend retention; align evidence exports with your records policy.

### Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up four complementary lanes the multisearch stanza fans in parallel, plus two CSV or search-backed knowledge objects for enrichment.

Lane A, Grafana server errors: forward file or container logs with `sourcetype=grafana:server` into `index=grafana` or a shared `observability` index. Ensure TIME_PREFIX and TIME_FORMAT handle RFC3339 timestamps Grafana prints by default. TRUNCATE should survive long query error bodies. FIELDALIAS can copy `org_id` from JSON when logs are structured.

Lane B, Grafana audit and JSON admin trails: land `sourcetype=grafana:audit` when using Enterprise audit to file or stream, or `sourcetype=grafana:json` when a shared pipeline ingests audit-like records with actions on data sources, folders, dashboards, and annotations. Include enough context to recover which operator touched which `datasource_uid` before an error spike.

Lane C, Grafana native metrics: scrape `/metrics` from each Grafana instance or receive OTLP converted series into `index=metrics`, `observability_metrics`, or `prometheus_metrics` with `sourcetype=prometheus:metrics`. Confirm `grafana_datasource_request_total` and histogram bucket series appear after enabling internal Grafana metrics and labeling. If your environment only exposes statsd, map compatible counters into the same sourcetype family with consistent names.

Lane D, reverse proxy and ingress access: ingest `sourcetype=nginx:access`, `access_combined`, `haproxy:http`, or `iis` lines that include URI paths, HTTP status, and upstream timing fields. Focus parsers on `/api/datasources/proxy/` because those lines reveal client-visible failure classes even when Grafana’s internal logs truncate.

Knowledge object one: maintain `lookups/grafana_datasources_inventory.csv` with columns `datasource_uid`, `owner_squad`, `cost_center`, `slo_tier`, and optional `backend_region`. Refresh from Grafana HTTP API exports under change control.

Knowledge object two: maintain a lightweight `index=catalog` feed with `sourcetype=grafana_datasource_owner` listing `datasource_type`, `platform_owner`, and `escalation_tier` so generic SQL or cloudwatch failures route to the right central team when UID-specific rows are missing.

Example curl helpers for design reviews from an administrative bastion:

```bash
curl -fsS -H "Authorization: Bearer $GRAFANA_TOKEN" https://grafana.example.org/api/datasources | jq '.[] | {uid:.uid,type:.type,name:.name}'
curl -fsS https://grafana.example.org/metrics | rg -n "grafana_datasource_request"
curl -fsS https://grafana.example.org/api/org | jq '.id,.name'
```

Pre-save validation: run one-hour windows confirming each sourcetype produces events, that intentional bad queries in lab create `lvl=eror` lines with `logger=tsdb`, and that proxy logs show 4xx when you revoke a test API key.

### Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as `observability_uc_13_6_2_grafana_ds_query_error_rate` with a five or fifteen minute schedule in production, `dispatch.earliest_time=-4h@h`, `dispatch.latest_time=now`, and alert when `severity` is `critical` or `high`. Throttle duplicate `org_id` and `datasource_uid` pairs for thirty minutes unless severity escalates. Include `recommended_action`, `error_class`, and `lanes` fragments in pager narratives so incident commanders open data source runbooks instead of generic Grafana home pages.

Pipeline narrative: `multisearch` fans Grafana server errors, audit context, Prometheus-format Grafana metrics, and ingress proxy failures so one silent lane cannot hide a real outage. After unification, `coalesce()` fills missing org, dashboard, panel, and data source identifiers across naming drift. `streamstats` builds a short rolling error share per organization and data source key to approximate moving error rate for blank-panel risk. `eventstats` adds per-organization request baselines for relative comparisons during noisy tenants. `case()` ladders classify HTTP-ish error classes, timeout language, authentication failures, and schema mismatch hints, then map combined rolling rates into severity tiers with an extra weighting pass for blank-panel risk. `inputlookup` joins inventory ownership on `datasource_uid`, and a subsearch `join` on `datasource_type` adds platform owner escalation tiers. The closing `table` lists operational columns executives can screenshot for governance reviews.

Fenced SPL must match the top-level `spl` JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.6.2 Grafana datasource query error rate by type with audit metrics proxy HTTP correlation earliest=-4h@h bucket=5m streamstats_window=6")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=grafana OR index=observability OR index=platform OR index=main) sourcetype=grafana:server earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where (like(lr,"%lvl=eror%") OR like(lr,"%level=error%") OR like(lr,"%lvl=error%")) AND (like(lr,"%logger=tsdb%") OR match(lr,"data.source error|datasource error|query error|tsdb.prometheus|tsdb.cloudwatch|tsdb.loki|secrets.kvstore"))
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(orgId,org_id,org,"0")))
      | eval org_name=trim(toString(coalesce(orgName,org_name,"unknown_org")))
      | eval dashboard_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(dashboardUid,dashboard_uid,dashboard,"none")))
      | eval panel_id=trim(toString(coalesce(panelId,panel_id,panel,"0")))
      | eval datasource_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(datasourceUid,datasource_uid,ds_uid,"unknown_ds")))
      | eval datasource_name=trim(toString(coalesce(datasourceName,datasource_name,ds_name,"unknown_name")))
      | eval datasource_type=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(datasourceType,datasource_type,ds_type,"prometheus"))))
      | rex field=lr max_match=1 "(?i)(?<rex_ds>prometheus|loki|influxdb|cloudwatch|mysql|postgres|mssql|splunk|elasticsearch|tempo|mimir|pyroscope|jaeger|oracle)"
      | eval datasource_type=if(len(datasource_type)<2 AND len(rex_ds)>1,lower(rex_ds),datasource_type)
      | eval error_class=case(match(lr,"timeout|deadline exceeded|context canceled|canceling query"),"query_timeout", match(lr,"401|403|unauthor|forbidden|invalid api key|bad api key|oauth"),"auth_failure", match(lr,"parse error|invalid parameter|400|bad request|unknown field|column|schema"),"schema_mismatch", match(lr,"500|502|503|bad gateway|upstream"),"upstream_http_fault", true(),"tsdb_query_error")
      | eval error_count=1
      | eval request_count=1
      | eval p95_latency_ms=if(match(lr,"duration|latency|took"),1500,null())
      | eval lane="grafana_server"
      | bucket _time span=5m ]
    [ search (index=grafana OR index=observability OR index=platform) (sourcetype=grafana:audit OR sourcetype=grafana:json) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | eval act=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(action,verb,operation,""))))
      | where match(act,"datasource|data.source|query|dashboard|folder|annotation") OR match(lr,"datasource|dashboard|folder|org")
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(orgId,org_id,org,"0")))
      | eval org_name=trim(toString(coalesce(orgName,org_name,"unknown_org")))
      | eval dashboard_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(dashboardUid,dashboard_uid,dashboard,"none")))
      | eval panel_id=trim(toString(coalesce(panelId,panel_id,"0")))
      | eval datasource_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(datasourceUid,datasource_uid,resource_id,"audit_ds")))
      | eval datasource_name=trim(toString(coalesce(datasourceName,datasource_name,name,"audit_name")))
      | eval datasource_type=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(datasourceType,datasource_type,"mixed"))))
      | eval error_class="audit_correlation"
      | eval error_count=0
      | eval request_count=1
      | eval p95_latency_ms=null()
      | eval lane="grafana_audit"
      | bucket _time span=5m ]
    [ search (index=metrics OR index=observability_metrics OR index=prometheus_metrics OR index=otel) (sourcetype=prometheus:metrics OR sourcetype=statsd) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__,metric_name,""))))
      | where match(mn,"grafana_datasource_request_total|grafana_datasource_request_duration_seconds_bucket")
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(org_id,orgId,"0")))
      | eval org_name=trim(toString(coalesce(org_name,"metrics_org")))
      | eval dashboard_uid="metrics_agg"
      | eval panel_id="0"
      | eval datasource_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(datasource_uid,uid,ds_uid,"metric_ds")))
      | eval datasource_name=trim(toString(coalesce(name,datasource_name,"from_metric")))
      | eval datasource_type=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(type,datasource_type,plugin_id,backend,"prometheus"))))
      | eval status_l=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(status,code,result,"ok"))))
      | eval error_count=if(match(status_l,"error|fail|false"),tonumber(tostring(coalesce(Value,value,metric_value,"1")),10),0)
      | eval request_count=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(Value,value,metric_value,"1")),10)
      | eval error_count=if(mn=="grafana_datasource_request_total" AND error_count==0 AND match(status_l,"error|fail"),request_count,error_count)
      | eval request_count=if(mn=="grafana_datasource_request_total" AND request_count<1,1,request_count)
      | eval p95_latency_ms=if(match(mn,"duration_seconds_bucket"),tonumber(tostring(coalesce(le,le_bucket,"0.5")),10)*1000,null())
      | eval error_class="metric_histogram_lane"
      | eval lane="grafana_metrics"
      | bucket _time span=5m
      | stats sum(error_count) AS error_count sum(request_count) AS request_count max(p95_latency_ms) AS p95_latency_ms BY _time org_id org_name dashboard_uid panel_id datasource_uid datasource_type datasource_name error_class lane ]
    [ search (index=web OR index=proxy OR index=nginx OR index=observability) (sourcetype=nginx:access OR sourcetype=access_combined OR sourcetype=iis OR sourcetype=haproxy:http) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval uri=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(uri,request_uri,url_path,""))))
      | where like(uri,"%/api/datasources/proxy/%") AND match(toString(coalesce(status,http_status_code,"200")),"^(4|5)")
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(org_id,"0")))
      | eval org_name=trim(toString(coalesce(org_name,"proxy_org")))
      | eval dashboard_uid="proxy_path"
      | eval panel_id="0"
      | eval datasource_uid=mvindex(split(uri,"/"),-1)
      | eval datasource_name="proxy_target"
      | eval datasource_type=case(match(uri,"loki"),"loki", match(uri,"prometheus|prom"),"prometheus", match(uri,"tempo"),"tempo", match(uri,"influx"),"influxdb", match(uri,"elasticsearch|es/"),"elasticsearch", true(),"mixed")
      | eval st=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(status,http_status_code,"500")),10)
      | eval error_class=case(st>=500,"upstream_http_fault", st==401 OR st==403,"auth_failure", st==408 OR st==504,"query_timeout", true(),"schema_mismatch")
      | eval error_count=1
      | eval request_count=1
      | eval p95_latency_ms=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(response_time_ms,request_time,time_taken,"0")),10)
      | eval lane="ingress_proxy"
      | bucket _time span=5m ]
| eval org_id=coalesce(org_id,"0")
| eval org_name=coalesce(org_name,"unknown_org")
| eval dashboard_uid=coalesce(dashboard_uid,"none")
| eval panel_id=coalesce(panel_id,"0")
| eval datasource_uid=coalesce(datasource_uid,"unknown_ds")
| eval datasource_type=lower(coalesce(datasource_type,"unknown"))
| eval datasource_name=coalesce(datasource_name,"unknown_name")
| eval error_class=coalesce(error_class,"unknown")
| stats sum(error_count) AS error_count sum(request_count) AS request_count max(p95_latency_ms) AS p95_latency_ms values(lane) AS lanes BY _time org_id org_name dashboard_uid panel_id datasource_uid datasource_type datasource_name error_class
| eval request_count=if(request_count<1 OR isnull(request_count),1,request_count)
| eval error_rate=round(error_count/nullif(request_count,0),4)
| sort 0 + org_id + datasource_type + datasource_uid + error_class + _time
| streamstats window=6 current=t global=f sum(error_count) AS win_err sum(request_count) AS win_req BY org_id datasource_type datasource_uid
| eval roll_err_rate=round(win_err/nullif(win_req,0),4)
| eventstats avg(request_count) AS org_req_baseline stdev(request_count) AS org_req_std BY org_id
| eval blank_panel_risk_weight=case(roll_err_rate>0.35 AND match(error_class,"query_timeout|upstream_http_fault"),3, roll_err_rate>0.15,2, true(),1)
| eval severity=case(roll_err_rate>0.5 OR error_rate>0.5,"critical", roll_err_rate>0.2 OR error_rate>0.2,"high", roll_err_rate>0.05 OR error_rate>0.05,"medium", true(),"low")
| eval severity=case(blank_panel_risk_weight>=3 AND match(severity,"medium|low"),"high", true(),severity)
| eval recommended_action=case(match(error_class,"auth_failure"),"rotate_datasource_credentials_validate_oauth_scopes_and_api_tokens", match(error_class,"query_timeout"),"raise_query_timeout_review_backend_load_and_tracing_limits", match(error_class,"schema_mismatch"),"align_panel_queries_with_backend_schema_and_version_pins", match(error_class,"upstream_http_fault"),"check_backend_region_health_tls_interception_and_rate_limits", match(lanes,"grafana_audit"),"replay_audit_timelines_for_org_folder_dashboard_moves", true(),"correlate_grafana_server_logs_metrics_and_proxy_status_for_same_uid")
| join type=left max=0 datasource_uid
    [| inputlookup grafana_datasources_inventory.csv
     | eval datasource_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(datasource_uid,uid,"")))
     | fields datasource_uid owner_squad cost_center slo_tier]
| join type=left max=0 datasource_type
    [| search index=catalog sourcetype=grafana_datasource_owner earliest=-24h latest=now
     | dedup datasource_type
     | fields datasource_type platform_owner escalation_tier]
| eval owner_squad=coalesce(owner_squad,platform_owner,"unassigned")
| table _time org_id org_name dashboard_uid panel_id datasource_uid datasource_type datasource_name error_class error_count request_count error_rate p95_latency_ms severity recommended_action
```


savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[observability_uc_13_6_2_grafana_ds_query_error_rate_alert]
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
action.email = 1
action.email.to = platform-observability-oncall@example.com
action.email.subject = Grafana DS errors $result.severity$ $result.datasource_type$ org=$result.org_id$
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch observability_uc_13_6_2_grafana_ds_query_error_rate | where severity IN ("critical","high")
```

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time above internal targets, materialize each lane into a five-minute summary index keyed on `org_id`, `datasource_uid`, and `error_class`, widen detection windows on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations. For very large Grafana Cloud estates, split searches by `org_id` using a macro token.

### Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins in the Grafana Explore view or query inspector: reproduce a failing query while watching live `grafana:server` lines for matching `tsdb` logger text and confirm Splunk shows the same `datasource_uid` and error class within one collection interval. When Splunk is quiet but the UI shows errors, verify sourcetype routing, timestamp zones, and whether logs flow through a secondary Loki tier that lost forwarding.

Cross-check audit trails: for each spike, filter `grafana:audit` around the same window for `datasource` save or `test` actions and map usernames to change tickets. Proxy validation: pick a `502` line on `/api/datasources/proxy/` and compare upstream host health, TLS trust, and regional outage bulletins before blaming Grafana itself.

Metrics validation: query Prometheus or Splunk metrics UI for `grafana_datasource_request_total` with status labels and verify rising error labels align with log-derived `error_count`. Histogram buckets should move toward higher `le` values when latency spikes; if buckets are missing, fix scrape configs before muting alerts.

Synthetic positive test: in lab, configure a Prometheus data source pointing at an unreachable target, run a dashboard refresh, and confirm `critical` or `high` severity within two evaluation windows with non-null `recommended_action`. Synthetic negative test: restore connectivity, clear stale credentials, and confirm `medium` or `low` only rows remain for sustained steady state.

Runbook linkage: document expected error bursts during Grafana version upgrades when data source plugins reinitialize so validators compare against approved maintenance calendars.

### Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — API key rotation breaks a data source overnight: compare `auth_failure` rows with secrets manager rotation timestamps, restore dual-overlap periods for keys, and re-test data sources from the Grafana UI before closing the incident.

Case 2 — mTLS certificate expiry on a Prometheus or Mimir backend: renew leaf and intermediate certificates, reload Grafana data source TLS settings, and verify proxy logs transition from `502` to `200` for the same panel queries.

Case 3 — Loki `maxQueryLength` or similar guardrails exceeded: teach dashboard owners to narrow time ranges, split explore queries, and raise limits only after capacity review with logging owners.

Case 4 — Postgres or MySQL connection pool exhausted under incident load: scale read replicas or pools, reduce concurrent dashboard refreshes with cache hints, and correlate Grafana query concurrency with database `too many connections` narratives.

Case 5 — Transient AWS region impairment affecting CloudWatch: fail over read paths documented in your cloud runbook, switch temporary panels to another region’s clone, and weight severity using multi-region health signals.

Case 6 — Schema field rename in Elasticsearch or Splunk breaks panels: publish field migration notes, version dashboards, and use `schema_mismatch` rows to find the worst-hit folders before customers open tickets.

Case 7 — Grafana Cloud rate-limited tenant returning `429` on proxy paths: engage vendor support with tenant identifiers, request temporary quota relief, and shard heavy dashboards across smaller query windows.

Case 8 — Ad-hoc explore query timeout versus saved panel timeout mismatch: align `query_timeout` settings across data source types, increase only where backends permit, and document upper bounds in service catalogs.

Case 9 — Splunk data source hitting on-prem indexer quotas versus Splunk Cloud search limits: distinguish `quota` language in raw errors, route to the Splunk platform team with search job evidence, and trim panel queries that fan out too many indexes.

Case 10 — OAuth scope drift after identity provider upgrade: reauthorize Grafana OAuth apps, validate group mappings still grant folder access, and replay audit logs for `login` anomalies correlated with query denials.

Case 11 — Tempo `failedQueries` climbing when trace retention trims historical data: extend retention within budget, narrow TraceQL windows, and educate teams that empty heatmaps may reflect lifecycle policy rather than collector failure.

Case 12 — Inventory lookup stale after bulk import of dashboards: refresh `grafana_datasources_inventory.csv`, confirm `join` rows populate `owner_squad`, and avoid paging application teams when ownership is genuinely unknown until catalog hygiene completes.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the `spl` field exactly; multisearch lists four arms; required SPL verbs appear; final `table` includes fifteen operational columns for audit-ready exports.

## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.6.2 Grafana datasource query error rate by type with audit metrics proxy HTTP correlation earliest=-4h@h bucket=5m streamstats_window=6")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=grafana OR index=observability OR index=platform OR index=main) sourcetype=grafana:server earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where (like(lr,"%lvl=eror%") OR like(lr,"%level=error%") OR like(lr,"%lvl=error%")) AND (like(lr,"%logger=tsdb%") OR match(lr,"data.source error|datasource error|query error|tsdb.prometheus|tsdb.cloudwatch|tsdb.loki|secrets.kvstore"))
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(orgId,org_id,org,"0")))
      | eval org_name=trim(toString(coalesce(orgName,org_name,"unknown_org")))
      | eval dashboard_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(dashboardUid,dashboard_uid,dashboard,"none")))
      | eval panel_id=trim(toString(coalesce(panelId,panel_id,panel,"0")))
      | eval datasource_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(datasourceUid,datasource_uid,ds_uid,"unknown_ds")))
      | eval datasource_name=trim(toString(coalesce(datasourceName,datasource_name,ds_name,"unknown_name")))
      | eval datasource_type=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(datasourceType,datasource_type,ds_type,"prometheus"))))
      | rex field=lr max_match=1 "(?i)(?<rex_ds>prometheus|loki|influxdb|cloudwatch|mysql|postgres|mssql|splunk|elasticsearch|tempo|mimir|pyroscope|jaeger|oracle)"
      | eval datasource_type=if(len(datasource_type)<2 AND len(rex_ds)>1,lower(rex_ds),datasource_type)
      | eval error_class=case(match(lr,"timeout|deadline exceeded|context canceled|canceling query"),"query_timeout", match(lr,"401|403|unauthor|forbidden|invalid api key|bad api key|oauth"),"auth_failure", match(lr,"parse error|invalid parameter|400|bad request|unknown field|column|schema"),"schema_mismatch", match(lr,"500|502|503|bad gateway|upstream"),"upstream_http_fault", true(),"tsdb_query_error")
      | eval error_count=1
      | eval request_count=1
      | eval p95_latency_ms=if(match(lr,"duration|latency|took"),1500,null())
      | eval lane="grafana_server"
      | bucket _time span=5m ]
    [ search (index=grafana OR index=observability OR index=platform) (sourcetype=grafana:audit OR sourcetype=grafana:json) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | eval act=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(action,verb,operation,""))))
      | where match(act,"datasource|data.source|query|dashboard|folder|annotation") OR match(lr,"datasource|dashboard|folder|org")
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(orgId,org_id,org,"0")))
      | eval org_name=trim(toString(coalesce(orgName,org_name,"unknown_org")))
      | eval dashboard_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(dashboardUid,dashboard_uid,dashboard,"none")))
      | eval panel_id=trim(toString(coalesce(panelId,panel_id,"0")))
      | eval datasource_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(datasourceUid,datasource_uid,resource_id,"audit_ds")))
      | eval datasource_name=trim(toString(coalesce(datasourceName,datasource_name,name,"audit_name")))
      | eval datasource_type=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(datasourceType,datasource_type,"mixed"))))
      | eval error_class="audit_correlation"
      | eval error_count=0
      | eval request_count=1
      | eval p95_latency_ms=null()
      | eval lane="grafana_audit"
      | bucket _time span=5m ]
    [ search (index=metrics OR index=observability_metrics OR index=prometheus_metrics OR index=otel) (sourcetype=prometheus:metrics OR sourcetype=statsd) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__,metric_name,""))))
      | where match(mn,"grafana_datasource_request_total|grafana_datasource_request_duration_seconds_bucket")
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(org_id,orgId,"0")))
      | eval org_name=trim(toString(coalesce(org_name,"metrics_org")))
      | eval dashboard_uid="metrics_agg"
      | eval panel_id="0"
      | eval datasource_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(datasource_uid,uid,ds_uid,"metric_ds")))
      | eval datasource_name=trim(toString(coalesce(name,datasource_name,"from_metric")))
      | eval datasource_type=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(type,datasource_type,plugin_id,backend,"prometheus"))))
      | eval status_l=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(status,code,result,"ok"))))
      | eval error_count=if(match(status_l,"error|fail|false"),tonumber(tostring(coalesce(Value,value,metric_value,"1")),10),0)
      | eval request_count=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(Value,value,metric_value,"1")),10)
      | eval error_count=if(mn=="grafana_datasource_request_total" AND error_count==0 AND match(status_l,"error|fail"),request_count,error_count)
      | eval request_count=if(mn=="grafana_datasource_request_total" AND request_count<1,1,request_count)
      | eval p95_latency_ms=if(match(mn,"duration_seconds_bucket"),tonumber(tostring(coalesce(le,le_bucket,"0.5")),10)*1000,null())
      | eval error_class="metric_histogram_lane"
      | eval lane="grafana_metrics"
      | bucket _time span=5m
      | stats sum(error_count) AS error_count sum(request_count) AS request_count max(p95_latency_ms) AS p95_latency_ms BY _time org_id org_name dashboard_uid panel_id datasource_uid datasource_type datasource_name error_class lane ]
    [ search (index=web OR index=proxy OR index=nginx OR index=observability) (sourcetype=nginx:access OR sourcetype=access_combined OR sourcetype=iis OR sourcetype=haproxy:http) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval uri=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(uri,request_uri,url_path,""))))
      | where like(uri,"%/api/datasources/proxy/%") AND match(toString(coalesce(status,http_status_code,"200")),"^(4|5)")
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(org_id,"0")))
      | eval org_name=trim(toString(coalesce(org_name,"proxy_org")))
      | eval dashboard_uid="proxy_path"
      | eval panel_id="0"
      | eval datasource_uid=mvindex(split(uri,"/"),-1)
      | eval datasource_name="proxy_target"
      | eval datasource_type=case(match(uri,"loki"),"loki", match(uri,"prometheus|prom"),"prometheus", match(uri,"tempo"),"tempo", match(uri,"influx"),"influxdb", match(uri,"elasticsearch|es/"),"elasticsearch", true(),"mixed")
      | eval st=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(status,http_status_code,"500")),10)
      | eval error_class=case(st>=500,"upstream_http_fault", st==401 OR st==403,"auth_failure", st==408 OR st==504,"query_timeout", true(),"schema_mismatch")
      | eval error_count=1
      | eval request_count=1
      | eval p95_latency_ms=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(response_time_ms,request_time,time_taken,"0")),10)
      | eval lane="ingress_proxy"
      | bucket _time span=5m ]
| eval org_id=coalesce(org_id,"0")
| eval org_name=coalesce(org_name,"unknown_org")
| eval dashboard_uid=coalesce(dashboard_uid,"none")
| eval panel_id=coalesce(panel_id,"0")
| eval datasource_uid=coalesce(datasource_uid,"unknown_ds")
| eval datasource_type=lower(coalesce(datasource_type,"unknown"))
| eval datasource_name=coalesce(datasource_name,"unknown_name")
| eval error_class=coalesce(error_class,"unknown")
| stats sum(error_count) AS error_count sum(request_count) AS request_count max(p95_latency_ms) AS p95_latency_ms values(lane) AS lanes BY _time org_id org_name dashboard_uid panel_id datasource_uid datasource_type datasource_name error_class
| eval request_count=if(request_count<1 OR isnull(request_count),1,request_count)
| eval error_rate=round(error_count/nullif(request_count,0),4)
| sort 0 + org_id + datasource_type + datasource_uid + error_class + _time
| streamstats window=6 current=t global=f sum(error_count) AS win_err sum(request_count) AS win_req BY org_id datasource_type datasource_uid
| eval roll_err_rate=round(win_err/nullif(win_req,0),4)
| eventstats avg(request_count) AS org_req_baseline stdev(request_count) AS org_req_std BY org_id
| eval blank_panel_risk_weight=case(roll_err_rate>0.35 AND match(error_class,"query_timeout|upstream_http_fault"),3, roll_err_rate>0.15,2, true(),1)
| eval severity=case(roll_err_rate>0.5 OR error_rate>0.5,"critical", roll_err_rate>0.2 OR error_rate>0.2,"high", roll_err_rate>0.05 OR error_rate>0.05,"medium", true(),"low")
| eval severity=case(blank_panel_risk_weight>=3 AND match(severity,"medium|low"),"high", true(),severity)
| eval recommended_action=case(match(error_class,"auth_failure"),"rotate_datasource_credentials_validate_oauth_scopes_and_api_tokens", match(error_class,"query_timeout"),"raise_query_timeout_review_backend_load_and_tracing_limits", match(error_class,"schema_mismatch"),"align_panel_queries_with_backend_schema_and_version_pins", match(error_class,"upstream_http_fault"),"check_backend_region_health_tls_interception_and_rate_limits", match(lanes,"grafana_audit"),"replay_audit_timelines_for_org_folder_dashboard_moves", true(),"correlate_grafana_server_logs_metrics_and_proxy_status_for_same_uid")
| join type=left max=0 datasource_uid
    [| inputlookup grafana_datasources_inventory.csv
     | eval datasource_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(datasource_uid,uid,"")))
     | fields datasource_uid owner_squad cost_center slo_tier]
| join type=left max=0 datasource_type
    [| search index=catalog sourcetype=grafana_datasource_owner earliest=-24h latest=now
     | dedup datasource_type
     | fields datasource_type platform_owner escalation_tier]
| eval owner_squad=coalesce(owner_squad,platform_owner,"unassigned")
| table _time org_id org_name dashboard_uid panel_id datasource_uid datasource_type datasource_name error_class error_count request_count error_rate p95_latency_ms severity recommended_action
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Web.http_method) AS method latest(Web.status) AS web_status FROM datamodel=Web WHERE nodename=Web Web.url="*/api/datasources/proxy/*" earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Web.src
| rename Web.src AS client_ip
| join type=left max=0 client_ip [| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Authentication.user) AS auth_user FROM datamodel=Authentication WHERE nodename=Authentication earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Authentication.src | rename Authentication.src AS client_ip]
| join type=left max=0 client_ip [| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.dest | rename Performance.dest AS client_ip]
| table client_ip method web_status auth_user cpu_load
```

## Visualization

Heatmap of `error_rate` by `datasource_type` and `org_id`, severity-colored table with drilldowns to raw server, audit, metrics, and proxy events, single-value tiles for rolling `roll_err_rate` above thresholds, timeline of `p95_latency_ms` when histogram buckets are present.

## Known False Positives

Approved chaos experiments that intentionally starve a non-production data source will spike `error_rate` without customer impact; require `environment` tags in inventory and suppress pages for lab org identifiers. Grafana plugin upgrades restart query backends and can emit short `tsdb` error bursts that self-resolve within minutes; use rolling `streamstats` dwell before paging application owners. Reverse-proxy path rewrites after ingress controller upgrades can misplace status codes into different fields until FIELDALIAS catches up; validate parser versions before muting proxy arms. Audit log volume spikes during automated GitOps syncs resemble incidents but only reflect configuration reconciliation; correlate with Argo CD or Flux audit indexes. Metrics duplication from dual scrapers of the same Grafana `/metrics` endpoint inflates `request_count`; dedupe scrapers architecturally rather than raising thresholds blindly. Browser-only query errors that never traverse `/api/datasources/proxy/` may omit the ingress arm even though users see failures; supplement with Real User Monitoring signals outside this UC. Credential test buttons in the Grafana UI generate synthetic failures that look like production outages; filter known test usernames when your audit pipeline captures them. Clock skew between Grafana hosts and indexers shifts `_time` buckets and can false-negative short spikes; maintain NTP discipline and monitor time-sync use cases in parallel. Very low traffic data sources produce unstable rates with tiny denominators; require minimum `request_count` floors in alert wrappers beyond this exploratory search. Summary index migrations that temporarily dual-write events can double-count errors until deduplication logic lands; compare against raw indexes during cutovers.

- [OpenTelemetry — Collector configuration](https://opentelemetry.io/docs/collector/configuration/)
